title: NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(i) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) (Human resources and access control) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.30: NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.30' running on index=training (role, completion_date), OktaIM2:log (deprovision events), nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS exports)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id [
    search index=training earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
| eval training_age=round((now()-strptime(last_training,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where training_age > 365 OR isnull(last_training)
| table role_name, user_id, last_training, training_age
description: Policies on human resources security — screening, training, and termination procedures — are part of holistic risk management. This use case ingests LMS completion for privileged-role staff, background-check status flags (non-PII aggregates), and leaver access revocation timing for NIS2-scoped roles.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes training. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Hash user identifiers in training feeds where possible; (2) require annual secure-development and IR training for NIS2 privileged roles; (3) alert managers on overdue training; (4) correlate HR termination dates with Okta deprovision latency (aggregate only); (5) export compliance percentages for audits. Indexes required: `index=training`; Lookups: `nis2_privileged_role_roster`, `nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv`.
visualization: Table (overdue training), Single value (% compliant), Bar chart (by role family), Timeline (completion waves). Key fields: `last_training`, `role_name`, `training_age`, `user_id`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud

knownFalsePositives: Operational noise during large maintenance windows, controlled exercises, or scope changes to critical entity lists.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS exports).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=training` (role, completion_date), `OktaIM2:log` (deprovision events), `nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Hash user identifiers in training feeds where possible; (2) require annual secure-development and IR training for NIS2 privileged roles; (3) alert managers on overdue training; (4) correlate HR termination dates with Okta deprovision latency (aggregate only); (5) export compliance percentages for audits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv
| join user_id [
    search index=training earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_training by user_id
]
| eval training_age=round((now()-strptime(last_training,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where training_age > 365 OR isnull(last_training)
| table role_name, user_id, last_training, training_age
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Human Resources Security Measures Evidence (Art. 21(2)(i))** — Policies on human resources security — screening, training, and termination procedures — are part of holistic risk management. This use case ingests LMS completion for privileged-role staff, background-check status flags (non-PII aggregates), and leaver access revocation timing for NIS2-scoped roles.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=training` (role, completion_date), `OktaIM2:log` (deprovision events), `nis2_privileged_role_roster.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553), HTTP Event Collector (HEC — LMS exports). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **training_age** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where training_age > 365 OR isnull(last_training)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: last_training, role_name, training_age, user_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue training), Single value (% compliant), Bar chart (by role family), Timeline (completion waves).
grandmaExplanation: We policies on human resources security — screening, training, and termination procedures — are part of holistic risk management.
